subject: Gaming
title: Super Mario 64 Invents 3D Gaming
description: A fast breakdown of Super Mario 64 Invents 3D Gaming for builders.

hook.text: Nobody knew how to play a 3D game in 1996. Mario 64 had to invent the rules from scratch.

buildup.text: Miyamoto spent months getting Mario to feel fun in 3D. He tested movements in an empty room first.

discovery.text: The N64 analog stick was designed specifically for this game. Hardware was built to serve the software.

twist.text: Mario 64 was so revolutionary that developers studied it for years to learn how to design 3D spaces.

climax.text: Every 3D game made since owes something to Mario 64's camera, movement, and level design.

punchline.text: He made 3D feel natural. Every 3D game followed his blueprint.

quiz.question: Why was the N64 analog stick created?
options: It was designed specifically for Super Mario 64's 3D movement
It was a leftover from a cancelled project
